Patch Set 5:

> What should I use for the linux lustre client? Currently I exported
 > account_page_dirtied() myself but is that the best thing to do?

James, i opened a ticket for this:

https://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/LU-13289

Generally, this could be challenging for Lustre client, ideally Lustre could be landed in upstream client again which will avoid this potential issues in the future.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Surprised by the performance hit.
I suspect there is something broken here ... the intent was for symbol_get to only ever be called once.
Using kallsyms_lookup_name on module load seems to be a cleaner way.
Similar to the usage here:
   https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/37613/
for apply_workqueue_attrs.

Patch Set 5:

Can you checkout https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/37686/ and see if that fixes the performance regression?
Thanks!

parents.subject: LU-10073 tests: skip test smoke for PPC

commit.subject: LU-13288 llite: avoid symbol_get() in ll_account_page_dirtied()
commit.message:
LU-13288 llite: avoid symbol_get() in ll_account_page_dirtied()

ll_account_page_dirtied() is hot path for buffer write and symbol_get()
is very heavy operation as it need preempt disabled or module_mutex which
hurts performance, see following benchmark numbers:

$ ior -w -t 1m -b 192g -e -o /scratch/testdir/file

c38ab030d0 LU-12904 build: account_page_dirtied is not exported

Max Write: 802.32 MiB/sec (841.30 MB/sec)

998a494fa9 LU-12861 libcfs: provide an scnprintf and start using it

Max Write: 2253.32 MiB/sec (2362.78 MB/sec)

We have a helper LB_CHECK_EXPORT to check wheather a function is
exported or not, which means symbol_get() could be avoided.

Notice, cgroup writeback support for newer kernel could be broken,
but that is not problem of this patch, we could address it in
seperated patch.

Test-Parameters: clientdistro=el8.1
Fixes: c38ab030d0 ("LU-12904 build: account_page_dirtied is not exported")
Change-Id: Icb0d66cddbd71878b2571e9a9f0d5264c7108c22
Signed-off-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
